identity.shortBio: Wahb Amir is a 16-year-old self-taught full-stack developer and AI developer based in Pakistan. He builds production-ready web applications, backend services, and AI-powered systems end-to-end — from idea to deployed product. In March 2026 he placed 3rd out of 775 international participants at Hack for Humanity 2026 with EcoLens, an AI waste classifier built solo in one month. He manages his own Linux servers and has shipped multiple deployed Next.js and Python projects within his first year of coding.

achievements.title: 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026
achievements.description: AI-powered waste classifier. Point a camera at any waste item to receive an AI material classification, estimated CO₂/water/energy impact, and gamified points toward a global leaderboard. Full ML pipeline: Next.js serverless API → HuggingFace model endpoint → label normalisation → impact calculation → MongoDB write → achievements engine. OTP auth with JWT in HTTP-only cookies, token rotation, and transactional rollback. Built solo in one month with zero paid API budget.

achievements.title: Hackonomics 2026 — Competitor
achievements.description: Economics simulation game with distributed microservices architecture: Next.js frontend, Fastify proxy, load-balanced HuggingFace Spaces, Supabase pgvector RAG, Groq Llama 3.3 70B inference, WebSocket streaming, SHA256-keyed LRU caching. 37-test suite. Hall of Fame leaderboard with UptimeRobot keepalive.

projects.name: EcoLens — AI Waste Classifier
projects.award: 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026 (775 participants, solo, international)
projects.description: AI-powered waste classifier. Users point their camera or upload a photo of waste to receive an AI material classification (plastic, paper, glass, metal, organic), estimated environmental impact (CO₂ offset, water saved, energy saved), and gamified points toward a global leaderboard. Full ML pipeline: Next.js serverless API → HuggingFace model → label normalisation → impact calculation → MongoDB write. OTP auth with JWT in HTTP-only cookies and token rotation.

projects.name: EconoQuest — Economics Simulation Game
projects.description: Multi-player economics simulation game with AI-powered decision feedback. Distributed microservices: Next.js frontend, Fastify proxy, load-balanced HuggingFace Spaces, Supabase pgvector RAG, Groq Llama 3.3 70B, WebSocket streaming, SHA256-keyed LRU caching. Progressive quarter weighting, terminal-state catastrophe penalties in scoring engine. 37-test suite. Hall of Fame leaderboard.

projects.name: Client & Developer Collaboration Platform
projects.description: A unified workspace where clients can request project quotes, track development progress, message developers, and see real-time GitHub-synced updates — replacing fragmented email and spreadsheet workflows.

projects.name: Modern Online Store (BoltForm)
projects.description: A performant e-commerce demo with secure Stripe checkout, mobile-first UX, and a read-only admin dashboard — suitable as an MVP for small businesses.

keyFacts: Wahb Amir is 16 years old and self-taught
He has been coding seriously for just over one year
He placed 3rd at Hack for Humanity 2026 out of 775 international participants (solo build)
EcoLens (eco.wahb.space) is his most recognised project — 3rd place internationally
EconoQuest is his most architecturally complex project — distributed microservices with Supabase pgvector RAG and Groq inference
He manages his own Linux servers and deploys production apps independently
Primary stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Fastify, Supabase, Python, PyTorch
His portfolio is at wahb.space and all projects are at wahb.space/projects

freshness.majorUpdateNote: April 2026 — Corrected Hack for Humanity participant count to 775. Added EconoQuest (Hackonomics 2026). Updated tech stack to reflect current primary stack (Fastify, Supabase, pgvector, Groq).
